resources.string: The app decides locally whether to launch onboarding or your main shell.
No AI or network calls are used for this decision.
Set up a stable profile before you enter the main shell.

This full-screen setup flow collects the personal details the app needs to derive your deterministic calorie baseline and save separate working-target guidance without mixing onboarding into the bottom-nav experience.
Your calorie baseline is derived locally using Mifflin-St Jeor plus activity and goal adjustments. Guidance is persisted separately so the app can keep the baseline auditable.
I can turn this profile into a working calorie target.
Your baseline is ready for guidance.
Your working target matches the baseline.
Enter your profile details, save them once, and I will explain how your current working calorie target relates to the deterministic baseline.
Your deterministic baseline is %1$d kcal/day. Save or resave your profile any time you want the assistant to refresh the working target and explanation.
I am reviewing your saved profile and generating an updated working target explanation now.
The saved baseline is still intact, but the assistant guidance refresh did not complete. Use retry to ask for the explanation again.
Use consistent units so the app can derive a stable baseline calorie target from this profile.
This completes your first-run setup and saves the profile plus deterministic baseline locally before you enter the main app shell.
Profile and deterministic calorie target saved locally.
Enter valid numeric values for age, height, and weight before saving.
This baseline is calculated by the app and stored on your profile. It remains the auditable formula output even when the working target differs.
Formula: Mifflin-St Jeor BMR, activity multiplier, and goal adjustment.
This guidance is stored separately from your profile. Its calorie target is the working target the app uses for progress tracking and future calorie-target UI.
Working target currently matches the deterministic baseline.
Working target refreshed separately from the deterministic baseline.
Working target reset to the deterministic baseline.
Keep your stored baseline and working-target guidance aligned by editing your profile in the same dedicated flow used for setup.
Refine your stored details without losing the app shell.
This editor remains outside the bottom navigation so profile changes still feel like a focused setup task rather than a crowded tab page.
Save your updated profile, refresh the deterministic baseline, and persist any updated working-target guidance.
This tab becomes your assisted meal-entry workspace.
Phase 2.5 only establishes the structure, hierarchy, and layout for future text logging work. No AI parsing or review flow is active yet.
Phase 3 will add natural-language meal entry, local-first recall, structured review cards, and confirmed save behavior here.
The screen is already shaped around the future meal-entry flow, even though logging behavior is still deferred.
This area will host the main logging composer and the evolving meal-entry conversation surface.
For now it stays intentionally quiet so Phase 2.5 can focus on structure rather than partial feature logic.
Structured meal review will appear in this destination once Phase 3 begins.
Future meal review cards will sit in the center of this flow before anything is written to Room.
The review gate is reserved for real structured drafts only, so no simulated save behavior is shown yet.
Prior foods and local-first matching stay part of the Log experience rather than becoming a separate top-level page.
Previously eaten foods will be referenced from here once the text logging core is implemented.
This preserves the product IA: Log owns creation, lookup, review, and confirmation as one flow.
Browse dates first, then open a selected day.
Phase 2.5 introduces the route structure for date browsing and Day Detail without pulling real calendar logic or analytics forward.
Day-specific meal history will live under Calendar Day Detail later instead of a separate History tab.
This placeholder month panel establishes the layout for future browsing without pretending real calendar behavior exists yet.
Calendar owns the path into Day Detail, where selected-day meal history will eventually live.
Ready to open Day Detail for %1$s.
This route is ready to hold one selected day of meals.
Future chronological meal cards, editing entry points, and day-level totals will be anchored here.
This placeholder exists now so Calendar can own history navigation structurally before deeper meal logic is implemented.
Past meals for the selected date will appear here in time order.
Breakfast, lunch, dinner, and snack cards will be grouped cleanly inside this page later.
This section reserves space for selected-day totals and supporting context.
Phase 2.5 keeps this structural only so the route exists without forcing future analytics logic into the app early.
Summaries and trends will land here later.
This shell establishes the destination, hierarchy, and spacing for insights without introducing real summaries or chart logic early.
The app now feels like a complete product shell even while the deeper insight behavior remains deferred.
Weekly and monthly narratives will eventually sit in this destination.
This section is reserved for the most important calorie-tracking patterns and future AI-generated summaries.
Charts and structured summary blocks will appear here once the roadmap reaches the insights phases.
For now the layout prioritizes clarity, spacing, and hierarchy over placeholder data.
This is the most complete screen after onboarding.
These values come from local persistence and continue to drive your deterministic baseline.
This section shows the separate working target and guidance metadata saved during onboarding.
Profile editing stays available as a dedicated full-screen flow, not a tab-embedded setup page.
